description.content:
Retrieves the guest user billing configuration for the tenant.
Controls whether the tenant pays for guest user access to
entitlement management resources.

**Note:** Returns 404 if the guest billing feature has not been
configured for the tenant.

response.name: Guest billing not configured for this tenant

description.content:
Updates the guest billing configuration for the tenant.

**Warning:** This endpoint modifies billing configuration.

Retrieves access packages managed through the ELM service.
Access packages bundle resources with policies that govern
who can request access and for how long.
Maximum number of results to return

response.name: List of access packages

Retrieves entitlement management catalogs. Catalogs are containers
for access packages and their associated resources and policies.

Retrieves the list of connected organizations. Connected organizations
represent external organizations whose users can request access to

Maximum number of results to return
Maximum number of results to return

Retrieves the global Identity Governance settings for the tenant,
including entitlement management configuration and default policies.

Updates the global Identity Governance settings.

**Warning:** This endpoint modifies tenant-level governance settings.

The Entra IGA (Identity Governance and Administration) API is used by the Microsoft
Entra portal (entra.microsoft.com → Identity Governance) for managing entitlement
management and governance administration, including:

  - Entitlement Management (Access Packages, Catalogs, Policies)
  - Connected Organizations
  - Billing Configuration (Guest User Billing)
  - Governance Settings and Administration

All endpoints are served from `elm.iga.azure.com` and require a valid
Azure AD bearer token with Identity Governance permissions.

**Note:** This specification was generated through browser traffic analysis of the
Entra portal. Many Identity Governance features use Microsoft Graph APIs directly
rather than this dedicated ELM API. This spec covers the non-Graph endpoints
observed during portal interaction.

Graph-backed Identity Governance features such as Lifecycle Workflows are
intentionally excluded from this legacy ELM specification.

Some endpoints may return 404 if the corresponding Entra ID Governance license
feature has not been enabled for the tenant.
